$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `roleplay_templates` repo (Phase-2).
One committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-roleplay-templates-fixture.ts +
roleplay-templates-tier2.ts) and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness roleplay_templates tier-2 test).
THE HEADLINE: the first ARRAY-OF-OBJECTS JSON column (`renderingPatterns`),
plus a nullable JSON-OBJECT column (`dialogueDetection`). Both are stored as
compact JSON whose object key order is the Zod schema field order, so the
Rust port models them with typed serde structs in schema order (NOT
serde_json::Value, which would re-sort keys). The corpus varies the optional
fields on renderingPatterns elements (flags / scope / hideDelimiters present
vs absent) to exercise the omit behavior, and exercises dialogueDetection
both null and non-null.
SCOPE DODGES (no marshaling coverage lost): `delimiters` is held EMPTY ([])
everywhere (its element schema is a preprocessed discriminated union), and
`narrationDelimiters` uses the plain STRING form (stored as TEXT, not JSON).
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / text-replacement-rules fixtures use. NOT the real Friday
pepper; safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

seed.name: Standard
seed.systemPrompt: Use *actions* in asterisks and "dialogue" in quotes.

ops.comment: User template with varied-optional renderingPatterns (one bare, one with flags+scope+hideDelimiters) and a non-null dialogueDetection.
Minimal user template: EMPTY renderingPatterns and NULL dialogueDetection.
Replace name + the whole renderingPatterns array (now a single bare-optional pattern). createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Change only systemPrompt on the just-created `Curly` template. updatedAt pinned.
Delete the minimal `Plain` template created above.
